per_image.visible_evidence: The first usable closeup frame has an identifiable beaker rim/spout; red content is clearly present.
per_image.main_risk: Lower beaker/content reads like a solid red mass, weakening transparent-beaker credibility.

per_image.visible_evidence: Transparent beaker is visible; red material remains inside and a red blob/smear is outside on the tabletop, supporting leak/failed containment.
per_image.main_risk: Escaped material looks spherical/artifact-like, not clearly falling or spreading.

per_image.visible_evidence: Beaker and escaped red material are visible; marker view helps locate contained vs escaped material.
per_image.main_risk: Cyan/marker overlay is diagnostic, not product-facing native evidence.

per_image.visible_evidence: Tabletop layout and tiny beaker/red material positions are visible.
per_image.main_risk: Too distant for fluid evidence; large black band makes framing weak.

per_image.visible_evidence: Overhead layout shows tabletop and tiny red/grey objects.
per_image.main_risk: Too distant to identify transparent beaker or leak behavior clearly.

product_interpretation.explanation: The closeup end frame supports the failed-containment story, while wide native cameras are context-only and the marker video is diagnostic-only.

retake_recommendations: Capture a closer native-material end view with the escaped red material in frame and enough angle to show it outside the beaker wall.
Add an intermediate frame where material is visibly falling/leaving the beaker, not just already separated.
Fix the start-frame material/readability so the beaker remains visibly transparent instead of reading as a solid red object.
Keep wide cameras only as context shots; do not rely on them for fluid evidence.
Reduce black empty framing in camera1.
